chunk_summary: Levothyroxine dosing for pediatric hypothyroidism is age- and weight-dependent, with monitoring protocols to optimize thyroid function and clinical outcomes.
learning_objectives: Understand age- and weight-based levothyroxine dosing guidelines for pediatric hypothyroidism.
Learn administration techniques to improve levothyroxine absorption and avoid treatment failure.
Develop a monitoring plan for thyroid function and clinical outcomes in children with hypothyroidism.
key_facts: Infants require 8–15 micrograms/kg/day of levothyroxine, school-age children 4–6 micrograms/kg/day, and adolescents 2–4 micrograms/kg/day.
Levothyroxine should be administered daily at a consistent time, avoiding iron, calcium, and soy-based feeds to prevent absorption issues.
TSH and free T4 should be rechecked 4–6 weeks after therapy initiation or dose changes, then every 3–6 months once stable.
Lower initial doses may be used in severe hypothyroidism to reduce risks like pseudotumor cerebri.
Clinical monitoring includes growth velocity, pubertal progression, and school performance.
reference_quote: Indian Academy of Pediatrics Standard Treatment Guidelines recommend referral to pediatric endocrinology for infants, congenital hypothyroidism, very high TSH with low free T4, or persistent abnormal tests despite verified treatment adherence.
document_key_topics: Monitoring thyroid function and clinical outcomes
Impact of iodine deficiency on thyroid dysfunction
Administration and absorption considerations for levothyroxine
